Functions that do many things:

Are harder to reuse

Encourage reuse

Code Smells: Warning Signs in Software
A code smell is an indication that something may be wrong with the structure or design of the code.

Common Code Smells:
Very long functions or methods
Classes with too many responsibilities
Repeated code (duplication)
Excessive use of comments to explain logic
Deeply nested conditionals
Hard-coded values

Static analysis tools

KEY TAKEAWAYS
Session Summary
MT Evolution
Machine translation progressed from brittle rule-based
systems to data-driven statistical models to powerful
neural seq2seq architectures with attention
Summarization Approaches
Extractive methods guarantee factual accuracy while
abstractive methods produce more natural language at
the risk of hallucination
Coreference Importance
Resolving entity mentions across text is fundamental for
coherent understanding in downstream NLP tasks
Practical Application
Modern pre-trained models make advanced NLP

Why Clean Code Is a Core Part of Software Construction
Clean code is central to software construction because software systems:
Are built by teams, not individuals
Evolve continuously
Must be maintained long after the original developers leave
Are often business-critical
In poorly constructed systems:
Small changes cause unexpected failures
Developers fear touching existing code
Bugs increase as the system grows
Technical debt accumulates rapidly
Industry Reality: Clean Code at Scale
Google maintains billions of lines of code across thousands of repositories.

SESSION 14
Text Summarization
Automatic text summarization distills lengthy documents into shorter
versions while preserving the most important information.

Two Approaches to Summarization
Extractive Summarization
Select and copy the most important sentences from the
source document verbatim—like using a highlighter on a
textbook
Abstractive Summarization
Generate new sentences that capture key ideas in novel
phrasings—like a human writing summary notes
EXTRACTIVE METHODS
Extractive Summarization Techniques
Extractive methods rank sentences by importance and select the top-k.

ABSTRACTIVE METHODS
Abstractive Summarization
Abstractive approaches use sequence-to-sequence models (the same architecture as neural MT) to generate summaries.

The Evolution of Machine Translation

Sequence-to-Sequence Models
The Encoder

dense mathematical vector representation.

vector representation
Sequential Processing
RNN or Transformer layers capture
context and dependencies
Final State
Produce a fixed-size vector
encoding the entire sentence
The Decoder in Detail
The decoder generates the target sentence autoregressively—each word depends on all previously generated words.

Why Clean Code Is a Core Part of Software Construction
Clean code is central to software construction because software systems:
Are built by teams, not individuals
Evolve continuously
Must be maintained long after the original developers leave
Are often business-critical
In poorly constructed systems:
Small changes cause unexpected failures
Developers fear touching existing code
Bugs increase as the system grows
Technical debt accumulates rapidly
Industry Reality: Clean Code at Scale
Google maintains billions of lines of code across thousands of repositories.

Extractive Summarization
Select and copy the most important sentences from the

Abstractive Summarization
Generate new sentences that capture key ideas in novel
phrasings—like a human writing summary notes

Abstractive Summarization

EVALUATION
Measuring Translation Quality
The BLEU Score Challenge
Evaluating MT is notoriously difficult because there are many correct ways to translate any sentence.

The industry standard is BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy), which measures n-gram overlap between candidate and
reference translations.

How BLEU Works
Core Idea
Count how many 1-grams, 2-grams, 3-grams, and
4-grams in the machine translation appear in human
reference translations
Precision-Based
Rewards matching phrases
from reference
Brevity Penalty
Penalizes translations
shorter than reference
0-100 Scale
Higher scores indicate better quality
BLEU Limitations
While BLEU provides a quick, automatic evaluation metric that correlates reasonably well with human judgments, it has
significant drawbacks that practitioners must understand.
